author: @g7mks383
text: Merry Christmas to you all, I have learnt alot from your video's and find them thought provoking, I appreciate the time and effort put into making them.  While I agree with your words and I am not particularly religious or a Church goer are we not forgetting something. The clue is in "Christmas" we are suppose to be celebrating the birth  of Jesus. We appear to all be forgetting  what the day is all about. Even Christmas Cards now do not even show this instead have snow covered post-box's and robins anything except a Church or Stable and crib. The Advent Calendar is suppose to count how many days our left till Christmas Day not fill your face day. The school nativity has become socially unacceptable by teachers and parents who think we should have a show about snowmen or some other neutral item incase it may offend. No wonder children these days think of this time as how many presents they will get.  Merry Christmas to all.

author: @tdtrecordsmusic
text:
Merry Christmas Rob & everyone on your team. I've got to say that what Rob gives is typically what parents pass along to their children. Sometimes a really good job has an employer which is similar. Once I worked at a shop which restored the classic Citroen cars. I learned a great deal of craft there. It was only 10 or 15 years ago, thus there was much recycling/part restoration/fabrication took place)... 
    
         What you share Rob, is truly a blessing. I feel blessed to on this side of the screen :) 
Happy Holidays,
     - TDT

author: @bikerfirefarter7280
text:
Santa comes but once a year
that's why he's grinning ear to ear.
But this time, much to his chagrin
the Covid scare has kept him in.

Rudolph's hooter glows, it glows!
the biggest runny red red Rose.
Today he has to wear a mask,
Santa finds it such a task,

"A reindeer's face inside a bag?"
This Covid scare is such a drag.
He's not allowed to blow his nose,
In case the droplets soil your clothes.

The weather's not so festive either,
Will we ever get a breather?
And now his Mrs has the fear,
'Stay in tonight, it looks like; RAIN, DEAR'!

-------------------------------------
Merry Xmas Rob and all

author: @ArtisticImpressionsbyBobRouth
text: Merry Christmas to all at TNT.  You are so correct Rob. It's a time to remember and thank those who make a difference in our lives. I have told my kids and all who know me that life is a model of how we are immersed in energy. Our lives are like a spinning magnet inside an end less copper wire wrapping. The energy our field creates induces every other field within our reach or awareness. Some who are close to  us feel the strongest energy those farther away it is weaker yet still affects the farthest reaches, even other fields we don't know exist are moved by our actions.  We in this group have a left hand spin while many others are spinning to the right or maybe in a vertical axis.  We all affect everyone else at some level. We are a world within one field.  Merry Christmas to all.  Happiest of New Years.  Thanks Rob and Luke, your work does matter.

author: @Stuartrusty
text: Indeed so. Through what has been a challenging couple of years for many (me included) you and Luke have really fired up my engineering spirit once more. Now back in full time employment after nearly 18 months of job searching and doing bit part jobs to keep the funds coming in. I would personally like to say thank you to all of you (the seen and unseen) folk who make these videos possible and to all those who inspire the great spirit of engineering that this country is known for worldwide still. Much gratitude to you Rob and to Luke for keeping alive that great problem solving and solution driven creative force with a dash of fun and a healthy dose of enthusiasm that you are becoming known for. Wishing for you all and your loved ones a healthy and prosperous festive period continuing on into 2022. With grateful thanks.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Stuart that was a really nice thing to say and well put - thank you for taking the time and trouble to say it - bless your heart mate and all the best to you and yours

replies.author: @dave68
replies.text: @Stuart Russell - I would have to agree with you, albeit, I've not been out of work THAT long... But still the Pandemic is one reason I've lost my position. I do Wish you all the best, and again I thank @Robert Murray Smith for his insights and wonderful videos he has created. I have yet to become a millionaire from one of Roberts Ideas, but it may come soon. 😁
